title: Plimpton 322 PASS: 5 rows covering Rows 1,2,5,6,9 + QA anchor (2,1)
_fixture_purpose: General theorem witnesses. Every row of Plimpton 322 corresponds to a QA direction (d,e) with both d,e regular (5-smooth), generating triple (F,C,G)=(d²-e²,2de,d²+e²). The G/C ratio (Plimpton column 3÷column 2 = δ/β in Mansfield notation) terminates in sexagesimal because the denominator C=2de is a regular base-60 number. The column-1 value (G/C)² decreases from Row 1 to Row 15 (angle increases from ~45° to ~58° measured from the vertical). The QA fundamental direction (2,1) corresponds to Row 11 (unscaled triple 3,4,5). All rows: F²+C²=G² (Pythagorean); F,C,G>0 (SPVN no-zero = QA A1); I=C-F ≷ 0 distinguishes hyperbola/ellipse type.

general_theorem.claim: For any QA direction (d,e) with d,e regular (5-smooth): the triple (d²-e², 2de, d²+e²) forms a row of Plimpton-322 type; G/C terminates in base-60
general_theorem.proof_regularity: d,e regular → C=2de has only factors 2,3,5 → gcd(G,C) may reduce denominator but resulting denom still 5-smooth → G/C terminates in base-60
general_theorem.mansfield_wildberger: Mansfield & Wildberger 2017 (Historia Mathematica 44:395-419): Plimpton 322 is a trigonometric table in exact rational/sexagesimal arithmetic, not an approximation. The generating pairs (d,e) are chosen so both are regular, giving exact base-60 entries.
general_theorem.qa_chromogeometry: F=Qr(d,e) (red quadrance), C=Qg(d,e) (green quadrance), G=Qb(d,e) (blue quadrance) — Wildberger Chromogeometry Theorem 6: Qb²=Qr²+Qg², i.e. F²+C²=G²

witnesses._note: QA fundamental direction. Row 11 of Plimpton 322 encodes (45,60,75)=15×(3,4,5); unscaled direction (2,1)
Row 1 of tablet — largest G²/C² value = steepest angle

non_row_counterexample._note: Direction (7,3): d=7 is NOT regular (7 is prime, not 2/3/5-smooth). Triple (40,42,58)=2×(20,21,29). G/C=58/42=29/21; denom 21=3×7 — factor 7 makes it non-regular → G/C does NOT terminate in base-60. This is why (7,3) does NOT appear in Plimpton 322.
